description_long: This object is a group of six classical-style vases, each with distinct shapes and proportions, arranged in a row. The vases feature a variety of forms, including amphorae with twin handles, urns with wide mouths, and slender-necked vessels, all standing on small circular bases. The surfaces of the vases are matte and appear to be made from ceramic or terracotta, with subtle variations in earthy tones such as brown, gray, and green. The vases are unadorned by elaborate ornamentation, emphasizing their simple, utilitarian forms. Their sizes are relatively uniform, suggesting they are decorative rather than monumental. These vases would be well-suited for display in a public park, garden, or urban plaza as decorative elements.
description: A set of six matte, classical-style vases in earthy tones, each with unique shapes and simple, unadorned surfaces. The vases are arranged in a row and feature handles, wide mouths, and slender necks.
description_view_0: The vases are seen from the side, appearing stacked in a line with only their profiles and tops visible.
description_view_1: A clear frontal view of all six vases, showing their full shapes, handles, and openings.
description_view_2: The vases are viewed from the opposite side, with their backs aligned and only the tops and bases visible.
description_view_3: A rear view of the vases, displaying their backs and the symmetry of their forms and handles.
